TEXT.citation: Wikipedia contributors. (2026). Sakizaya Wikipedia. Wikimedia Foundation. Retrieved 2026-06-09, from https://szy.wikipedia.org/
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @misc{Wiki_szy, title = {Sakizaya Wikipedia}, author = {{Wikipedia contributors}}, year = {2026}, publisher = {Wikimedia Foundation}, url = {https://szy.wikipedia.org/}, urldate = {2026-06-09} }

S.FORM: == banic == ËÖπÁÄâ siwkay nu balulu u Pangcah a kamu sa , u mapiyas saan. adada' ku bili, besuc saan ku tayi', tanunanum saan ku tayi'. tinaku a kamu mabanic ËÖπÁÄâ pi:na tu a demiad mabanic kaku. mabanicay ËÖπÁÄâÁöÑ tadaaheteng ku nu mabanicay a tayi'. mibanic ÊúÉËÖπÁÄâ mibanic ku cucu nu katalalan. mibanicay ÊúÉËÖπÁÄâÁöÑ na u canan ku mibanicay i tisuwan? namabanic ËÖπÁÄâÈÅé‰∫Ü namabanic kaku i nacilal namukan tu cucu nu katalalan. sakibanic Ê≠¢ÁÄâËó• sakibanic ku mangazuway a papah nu kayabes. u sulit nu Hulam atu Amilika Diarrhea, also spelled diarrhoea, is the condition of having at least three loose or liquid bowel movements each day. It often lasts for a few days and can result in dehydration due to fluid loss. Signs of dehydration often begin with loss of the normal stretchiness of the skin and irritable behaviour. This can progress to decreased urination, loss of skin color, a fast heart rate, and a decrease in responsiveness as it becomes more severe.Loose but non-watery stools in babies who are breastfed, however, may be normal.
S.PHON: == bani ¶ == ** siwkaj nu bar~…æur~…æu u pa≈ã ¶aƒß a kamu sa , u mapijas saan. adada î~ ° ku bir~…æi, b…ôsu ¶ saan ku taji î~ °, tanunanum saan ku taji î~ °. tinaku a kamu mabani ¶ ** pi:na tu a d…ômiad mabani ¶ kaku. mabani ¶aj *** tadaaƒß…ôt…ô≈ã ku nu mabani ¶aj a taji î~ °. mibani ¶ *** mibani ¶ ku ¶u ¶u nu katar~…æar~…æan. mibani ¶aj **** na u ¶anan ku mibani ¶aj i tisuwan? namabani ¶ **** namabani ¶ kaku i na ¶ir~…æar~…æ namukan tu ¶u ¶u nu katar~…æar~…æan. sakibani ¶ *** sakibani ¶ ku ma≈ãazuwaj a papaƒß nu kajab…ôs. u sur~…æit nu ƒßur~…æam atu amir~…æika diarrƒß…ôa, ar~…æso sp…ôr~…ær~…æ…ôd diarrƒßo…ôa, is tƒß…ô ¶ondition of ƒßa*i≈ã at r~…æ…ôast tƒßr…ô…ô r~…æoos…ô or r~…æi*uid bow…ôr~…æ mo*…ôm…ônts …ôa ¶ƒß daj. it oft…ôn r~…æasts for a f…ôw dajs and ¶an r…ôsur~…æt in d…ôƒßjdration du…ô to fr~…æuid r~…æoss. signs of d…ôƒßjdration oft…ôn b…ôgin witƒß r~…æoss of tƒß…ô normar~…æ str…ôt ¶ƒßin…ôss of tƒß…ô skin and irritabr~…æ…ô b…ôƒßa*iour. tƒßis ¶an progr…ôss to d…ô ¶r…ôas…ôd urination, r~…æoss of skin ¶or~…æor, a fast ƒß…ôart rat…ô, and a d…ô ¶r…ôas…ô in r…ôsponsi*…ôn…ôss as it b…ô ¶om…ôs mor…ô s…ô*…ôr…ô.r~…æoos…ô but non-wat…ôrj stoor~…æs in babi…ôs wƒßo ar…ô br…ôastf…ôd, ƒßow…ô*…ôr, maj b…ô normar~…æ.
S.FORM: == banic == ËÖπÁÄâ siwkay nu balulu u Pangcah a kamu sa , u mapiyas saan. adada' ku bili, besuc saan ku tayi', tanunanum saan ku tayi'. tinaku a kamu mabanic ËÖπÁÄâ pi:na tu a demiad mabanic kaku. mabanicay ËÖπÁÄâÁöÑ tadaaheteng ku nu mabanicay a tayi'. mibanic ÊúÉËÖπÁÄâ mibanic ku cucu nu katalalan. mibanicay ÊúÉËÖπÁÄâÁöÑ na u canan ku mibanicay i tisuwan? namabanic ËÖπÁÄâÈÅé‰∫Ü namabanic kaku i nacilal namukan tu cucu nu katalalan. sakibanic Ê≠¢ÁÄâËó• sakibanic ku mangazuway a papah nu kayabes. u sulit nu Hulam atu Amilika Diarrhea, also spelled diarrhoea, is the condition of having at least three loose or liquid bowel movements each day. It often lasts for a few days and can result in dehydration due to fluid loss. Signs of dehydration often begin with loss of the normal stretchiness of the skin and irritable behaviour. This can progress to decreased urination, loss of skin color, a fast heart rate, and a decrease in responsiveness as it becomes more severe.Loose but non-watery stools in babies who are breastfed, however, may be normal.
S.PHON: == bani ¶ == ** siwkaj nu bar~…æur~…æu u pa≈ã ¶aƒß a kamu sa , u mapijas saan. adada î~ ° ku bir~…æi, b…ôsu ¶ saan ku taji î~ °, tanunanum saan ku taji î~ °. tinaku a kamu mabani ¶ ** pi:na tu a d…ômiad mabani ¶ kaku. mabani ¶aj *** tadaaƒß…ôt…ô≈ã ku nu mabani ¶aj a taji î~ °. mibani ¶ *** mibani ¶ ku ¶u ¶u nu katar~…æar~…æan. mibani ¶aj **** na u ¶anan ku mibani ¶aj i tisuwan? namabani ¶ **** namabani ¶ kaku i na ¶ir~…æar~…æ namukan tu ¶u ¶u nu katar~…æar~…æan. sakibani ¶ *** sakibani ¶ ku ma≈ãazuwaj a papaƒß nu kajab…ôs. u sur~…æit nu ƒßur~…æam atu amir~…æika diarrƒß…ôa, ar~…æso sp…ôr~…ær~…æ…ôd diarrƒßo…ôa, is tƒß…ô ¶ondition of ƒßa*i≈ã at r~…æ…ôast tƒßr…ô…ô r~…æoos…ô or r~…æi*uid bow…ôr~…æ mo*…ôm…ônts …ôa ¶ƒß daj. it oft…ôn r~…æasts for a f…ôw dajs and ¶an r…ôsur~…æt in d…ôƒßjdration du…ô to fr~…æuid r~…æoss. signs of d…ôƒßjdration oft…ôn b…ôgin witƒß r~…æoss of tƒß…ô normar~…æ str…ôt ¶ƒßin…ôss of tƒß…ô skin and irritabr~…æ…ô b…ôƒßa*iour. tƒßis ¶an progr…ôss to d…ô ¶r…ôas…ôd urination, r~…æoss of skin ¶or~…æor, a fast ƒß…ôart rat…ô, and a d…ô ¶r…ôas…ô in r…ôsponsi*…ôn…ôss as it b…ô ¶om…ôs mor…ô s…ô*…ôr…ô.r~…æoos…ô but non-wat…ôrj stoor~…æs in babi…ôs wƒßo ar…ô br…ôastf…ôd, ƒßow…ô*…ôr, maj b…ô normar~…æ.
